question.text: For resident Indian customers with an existing Financial Group relationship in IFSC, the CDD updation periodicity for a MEDIUM-RISK customer is:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For resident Indian customers with an existing Financial Group relationship, the medium-risk CDD updation periodicity is once in every 8 years. This is significantly longer than the 3-year periodicity applicable to medium-risk standard customers, reflecting the reduced risk due to the existing group relationship.
**The Trap:** "Once in 3 years" is the medium-risk periodicity for STANDARD customers, not resident Indians with Financial Group relationships. "Once in 5 years" is the low-risk standard customer periodicity. "Once in 10 years" is the LOW-risk resident Indian periodicity, not medium-risk. The 8-year figure is unusual and therefore easy to forget.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The difference between standard (3 years) and resident Indian (8 years) for medium-risk CDD is one of the most testable distinctions in the IFSCA-01 exam. Candidates must memorise both tiers: Standard = 1-3-5 and Resident Indian = 2-8-10.
**Key Takeaway:** Medium-risk CDD: Standard customer = 3 years vs. Resident Indian (with Financial Group) = 8 years. The 8-year figure is distinctive and frequently tested.
